mechanism: Central bank credibility determines whether inflation expectations are anchored, which is the primary discriminant between the low-passthrough regime (0.1-0.3pp) and high-passthrough regime (0.5-0.8pp). Credibility is the strongest cross-sectional predictor of passthrough outcomes.
regime_dependence: Relationship holds universally but the magnitude of credibility's effect is strongest when commodity shocks are large enough to potentially de-anchor expectations. Small shocks are absorbed regardless of credibility.

mechanism: Dollar trajectory determines realized commodity cost in local currency for every non-US country. A strengthening dollar amplifies passthrough for importers and partially self-hedges the US. The FX channel is the dominant transmission mechanism in EM (ERPT 0.15-0.40 vs DM 0.03-0.08).
regime_dependence: Always operative, but magnitude depends on ERPT coefficient which varies by country credibility. Most impactful during periods of dollar strengthening coincident with commodity price increases (the commodity-FX doom loop).

mechanism: The passthrough regime determines whether commodity shocks remain first-round (price-level shift) or activate second-round effects (wages, expectations). The high-passthrough regime is a necessary but not sufficient condition for second-round activation; institutional factors (union density, COLA coverage) must also be present.
regime_dependence: Second-round effects require both the high-passthrough regime AND institutional channels (union density >15%, COLA coverage, tight labor markets). In the low-passthrough regime, second-round effects are near-impossible regardless of institutional structure.

mechanism: Fiscal deficits sustain demand and prevent the natural demand-destruction that self-corrects commodity price spikes. This amplifies passthrough and can shift the economy from the low-passthrough to the high-passthrough regime by preventing the adjustment mechanism.
regime_dependence: Most impactful when deficits are large (>5% GDP) and deficit-funded spending has high marginal propensity to consume. Deficit composition matters more than headline ratio. Less impactful when deficits fund investment with lower immediate demand effects.

mechanism: Food inflation in EM directly de-anchors inflation expectations because food CPI weights are 3-4x higher and households form expectations from daily food purchases. This creates a credibility-FX feedback loop: food inflation → expectations drift → FX depreciation → more food inflation.
regime_dependence: Strongest in EM economies with food CPI weights >30%, limited food subsidies, and informal labor markets where wage demands respond to food prices. Weaker in EM economies with strong central bank credibility and food price stabilization mechanisms.

mechanism: Which commodity-inflation transmission channel dominates determines the stock-bond correlation sign. When second-round effects are absent (base case), bonds hedge equities (negative correlation). When second-round effects activate, Fed tightens aggressively and both stocks and bonds fall (positive correlation).
regime_dependence: Channel (a) transitory: negative correlation (2014-19 template). Channel (b) margin compression without spiral: near-zero (2015-16). Channel (c) wage-price spiral risk: positive correlation (2022). The transition between regimes can be abrupt.

mechanism: BOJ accommodating and PBOC administering while Fed/ECB tighten widens rate differentials, strengthens the dollar, and amplifies the FX channel for all other countries. The structural policy divergence is an amplifier of the dollar channel.
regime_dependence: Most impactful during synchronized global commodity shocks when policy divergence is widest. Less relevant during country-specific shocks. BOJ divergence was most extreme under YCC; may moderate with policy normalization.

mechanism: Discrete administered price adjustments cause sudden large inflation spikes that de-anchor expectations more effectively than gradual passthrough would. The political timing of subsidy removal creates correlated fiscal-FX-inflation shocks.
regime_dependence: Only operative in EM with administered energy/food pricing. Timing is politically determined and therefore unpredictable. Impact scales with the size of the accumulated subsidy gap at the point of adjustment.

mechanism: The tiered lag structure (energy → food → services) means first-round effects arrive sequentially across sectors, creating an inflation breadth signal that can be confused with genuine second-round broadening. This may cause central banks to over-tighten.
regime_dependence: Most likely to create confusion during large commodity shocks with long subsequent services lags (6-36 months). Less confusing when shocks are small and services inflation remains stable throughout.

mechanism: FIFO-driven margin overstatement provides the appearance of pricing power, which companies then protect through complementary non-GAAP adjustments. Both distortions compound to make reported earnings increasingly divorced from economic reality during commodity inflation.
regime_dependence: Both effects are mechanically linked to commodity price increases and reverse when prices decline. LIFO/FIFO is deterministic; non-GAAP widening involves managerial discretion and tends to persist longer (sticky adjustments).

mechanism: Both represent hidden valuation channels during commodity inflation invisible in standard metrics (P/E, EV/EBITDA). Working capital consumes cash not visible on income statement; LIFO/FIFO distorts the income statement itself. Together they make EV/FCF the appropriate metric.
regime_dependence: Both activate during commodity price increases and reverse during decreases, though working capital release may lag price declines due to inventory cycle timing.

mechanism: Fed credibility determines which USD loop operates. When the Fed is perceived as ahead of the curve, the self-correcting loop strengthens the dollar and moderates commodities. When behind the curve, the self-reinforcing loop weakens the dollar and amplifies commodities.
regime_dependence: The loop identification depends on real-time perception of Fed credibility. Transitions between loops can be abrupt when market consensus shifts (e.g., November 2021 when transitory was abandoned).

mechanism: The natural gas → fertilizer → crop costs → food prices channel adds an additional lag layer to commodity-inflation transmission, explaining why food inflation lags energy by more than transportation costs alone would predict.
regime_dependence: Strongest during natural gas price spikes due to fertilizer production costs. Less relevant for oil-only shocks where the energy-food link is primarily through transportation.

mechanism: With only 6-8 independent episodes, all parametric passthrough coefficient estimates (including the bimodal ranges) carry more uncertainty than typically reported. Directional signs are reliable but magnitudes should be discounted 2-3 confidence points.
regime_dependence: Always applicable to parametric estimates. Less applicable to directional claims (sign consistency across all episodes) and structural/institutional observations (union density, COLA coverage) which are independently verifiable.

mechanism: The higher sacrifice ratio for supply-driven inflation (1.5-2.5x) makes central bank look-through doctrine more attractive on cost-benefit grounds, but also harder to maintain when the three abandonment conditions are met.
regime_dependence: Most relevant during clearly supply-driven shocks. Less relevant when demand and supply components are mixed (as in 2021-22 where attribution is contested).
